title.es: Bias-Variance Tradeoff y Teoremas No Free Lunch
title.en: Bias-Variance Tradeoff and No Free Lunch Theorems

summary_es: Este modulo descompone el error de prediccion en sus tres componentes fundamentales: bias al cuadrado, varianza y ruido irreducible. Se demuestra visualmente el espectro de underfitting a overfitting usando polinomios de grado creciente en datos sinteticos financieros. Se presenta el teorema No Free Lunch de Wolpert (1996) que demuestra que ningun algoritmo es universalmente optimo y que el conocimiento del dominio es obligatorio para seleccionar modelos efectivos. Se conecta la regularizacion Ridge con priors bayesianos, mostrando que PML formaliza la integracion de conocimiento financiero como herramienta para navegar el tradeoff. Se aplican los conceptos a modelos financieros desde CAPM hasta redes neuronales.
summary_en: This module decomposes prediction error into its three fundamental components: squared bias, variance, and irreducible noise. The underfitting-to-overfitting spectrum is visually demonstrated using polynomials of increasing degree on synthetic financial data. Wolpert's No Free Lunch theorem (1996) is presented, proving that no algorithm is universally optimal and that domain knowledge is mandatory for effective model selection. Ridge regularization is connected to Bayesian priors, showing that PML formalizes financial knowledge integration as a tool for navigating the tradeoff. Concepts are applied to financial models from CAPM to neural networks.
learning_objectives_es: Descomponer el error de prediccion en bias^2, varianza y ruido irreducible
Visualizar underfitting y overfitting con polinomios de complejidad creciente
Demostrar el teorema No Free Lunch con multiples algoritmos y problemas
Conectar regularizacion Ridge con priors bayesianos de PML
Aplicar bias-variance a la seleccion de modelos financieros
learning_objectives_en: Decompose prediction error into bias^2, variance, and irreducible noise
Visualize underfitting and overfitting with polynomials of increasing complexity
Demonstrate the No Free Lunch theorem with multiple algorithms and problems
Connect Ridge regularization with PML Bayesian priors
Apply bias-variance to financial model selection

parameters.name: Trifecta of Modeling Errors
parameters.origin_phrase: Bias-variance decomposition reveals the fundamental tradeoff in all models

parameters.name: Specification Errors
parameters.origin_phrase: High bias models are misspecified; high variance models overfit noise

parameters.name: Knowledge Integration
parameters.origin_phrase: NFL theorems prove domain knowledge is mandatory, not optional

parameters.name: Model Robustness
parameters.origin_phrase: Priors reduce variance without destroying signal, improving robustness

parameters.name: Academic Arrogance
parameters.origin_phrase: Belief in a universally best algorithm ignores NFL theorems
